title: machino check --json output
description: Every machino CLI command that accepts --json emits exactly one JSON object on stdout. This schema covers `machino check --json` (also produced on failed loads by `test --json` and `query`). Positions are 1-based lines and columns.

ok.description: true when the program lexes, parses and type-checks.

functions.description: Present on success: number of user (non-std) functions.

structs.description: Present on success: number of user structs.

tests.description: Present on success: number of test blocks.

diagnostics.description: Present on failure: one entry per diagnostic.

severity.description: machino reports only hard errors; there are no warnings.

code.description: Stable error code (e.g. E035). Agents can key repair strategies on it; codes are never reused for a different meaning.

message.description: One-line human-readable description of the problem.

file.description: Path of the file the span points into (import-aware: spans are mapped back from the compiled bundle to the source file).

fix.description: Optional machine-applicable edit that resolves the diagnostic: replace the text between (line,col) and (endLine,endCol) with `replace`.
